context: Tests whether the agent applies the large-codebase reverse-engineering strategy: one-pass scan, feature clustering, end-to-end cluster processing, and late data-layer pass. Also checks entity type mapping to AIUP vocabulary, exclusion of infrastructure tables, UC ID ordering by actor then importance, and handling of partially-implemented features.

checklist.name: PLAN.md exists
checklist.description: A docs/PLAN.md file is present and non-empty

checklist.name: Controller file list
checklist.description: PLAN.md contains an explicit list of controller files found during the initial one-pass scan (e.g. AuthController, UserController, ProductController, CategoryController, OrderController, CartController, NotificationController, AdminUserController, AdminProductController, ReportController)

checklist.name: Feature clustering documented
checklist.description: PLAN.md groups the controller files into named feature clusters (e.g. auth, users, products, orders/cart, notifications, admin/reports) before any specs are written — showing that clustering happened as a pre-processing step

checklist.name: Excluded tables listed
checklist.description: PLAN.md names at least one infrastructure table excluded from the entity model (spring_session, spring_session_attributes, or audit_log) and gives a reason for exclusion

checklist.name: No SQL types in entity model
checklist.description: docs/entity_model.md does NOT contain raw SQL types such as bigint, varchar, numeric, smallint, or timestamp — only AIUP vocabulary types (Long, String, Integer, Decimal, Boolean, Date, DateTime)

checklist.name: AIUP types used
checklist.description: docs/entity_model.md uses at least three of the AIUP Data Types: Long, String, Integer, Decimal, Boolean, Date, DateTime

checklist.name: spring_session absent
checklist.description: docs/entity_model.md does NOT contain an entity section for spring_session or spring_session_attributes

checklist.name: audit_log absent
checklist.description: docs/entity_model.md does NOT contain an entity section for audit_log

checklist.name: UC IDs grouped by actor
checklist.description: Use case IDs (UC-001, UC-002, …) are assigned in actor groups — all IDs for one actor are consecutive before starting the next actor's block (e.g. Visitor UCs first, then Buyer UCs, then Artisan UCs, then Admin/Manager UCs)

checklist.name: Partial implementation noted
checklist.description: At least one use case spec includes a note or Draft status where the implementation appeared partial or unclear — rather than silently omitting the use case or inventing unsubstantiated flows

checklist.name: Endpoints aggregated into use cases
checklist.description: The number of use case files is fewer than the total number of controller methods (there are ~35 endpoints across all controllers) — confirming that endpoints were grouped by goal, not one-to-one

checklist.name: Entity model from migrations
checklist.description: docs/entity_model.md includes entities derived from the SQL migration files (e.g. USER, PRODUCT, ORDER, CATEGORY, REVIEW) — not just from the Java entity class names verbatim
